define i1 @or_operand_order_reversed(i1 %cond, i8 %x) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i8 2, i8 0
  %or = or i8 %x, %sel
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %or, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions m_c_Or allows operand order flexibility but all known examples show select first; this tests X or select pattern explicitly

  %or = or i8 %x, %sel
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %or, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}

entry:

  %cmp = or i1 %0, %cond
  ret i1 %cmp
}

tests.source_program:
define <2 x i1> @vector_i16_power_of_two(<2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i16> %x) {
entry:
  %sel = select <2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i16> <i16 8, i16 8>, <2 x i16> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <2 x i16> %sel, %x
  %cmp = icmp ne <2 x i16> %or, zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i1> %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows vector i32 and scalar i8 examples, but not vector i16 with power-of-two true value (8) - different element bitwidth not covered

alive2.src:
define <2 x i1> @vector_i16_power_of_two(<2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i16> %x) {
entry:
  %sel = select <2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i16> <i16 8, i16 8>, <2 x i16> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <2 x i16> %sel, %x
  %cmp = icmp ne <2 x i16> %or, zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i1> %cmp
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define <2 x i1> @vector_i16_power_of_two(<2 x i1> %cond, <2 x i16> %x) {
entry:
  %0 = icmp ne <2 x i16> %x, zeroinitializer
  %cmp = or <2 x i1> %0, %cond
  ret <2 x i1> %cmp
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @eq_predicate_zero_trueval(i1 %cond, i32 %x) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 0, i32 16
  %or = or i32 %sel, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes checking if zero matches TV or FV with NE predicate, but EQ with zero as true value creates different optimization path not explicitly documented
